commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer/product-security judgment about Slack OAuth installation, not a safe automated repair on this docs-only branch.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a Slack workspace authorization path with an unexplained client ID and no matching OpenClaw token-exchange or storage flow.; Review finding: [P1] Remove the unsupported OAuth install button — docs/channels/slack.md:87 (sha=7d17ca797c0406e3f5875ab8a28ebe9dbe3ecb71)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an Add to Slack OAuth button and a short manual-setup sentence to `docs/channels/slack.md`.

PR surface: Docs +4. Total +4 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection is enough: the PR adds a Slack OAuth redirect link, while current docs and runtime require OpenClaw-owned bot/app/signing credentials that the link does not provide.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer/product-security judgment about Slack OAuth installation, not a safe automated repair on this docs-only branch.

target.title: docs: add 'Add to Slack' OAuth button to Slack channel setup page
target.body: ## Summary Adds the official "Add to Slack" OAuth button to the top of the Quick setup section in `docs/channels/slack.md`. The button is placed right before the manual setup tabs so users can one-click install the app into their workspace without going through the manual app creation flow. ## Change - Added the `` + `` button block (using the official Slack platform CDN images at 1x/2x) immediately after the `## Quick setup` heading - Added a short "Or set up manually using the steps below." line to preserve the manual flow for users who need custom scopes or a self-hosted app ## OAuth URL The button uses the client ID and scope set provided: ``` client_id=10815128009479.10859195249184 scope=reactions:read,app_mentions:read,chat:write,commands,im:history,reactions:write ```
target.author: detached-cody
